name: Integrating Merchandising Cloud with Vue frontend on Shopify
body: [{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"Vue.js is becoming more and more popular among Shopify merchants to power the rendering of the frontend of the store."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"Sometimes the Vue.js setup is done in a way that it overrides existing Shopify HTML elements, which also affects Merchandising Cloud integration in cases when Vue.js is loaded after Merchandising Cloud. This issue can be hard to notice as by default there's no guaranteed order in which Merchandising Cloud or Vue.js scripts are loaded."}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Solution"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"After Merchandising Cloud creates and attaches it's widgets to the DOM elements, it keeps the references to the widgets in the config, so even if the DOM gets overridden it's possible to re-attach the widget to DOM again using the DOM API."}]},{"type":"code_block","children":[{"type":"code_line","children":[{"text":"// Vue component"}]},{"type":"code_line","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"code_line","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"code_line","children":[{"text":"\n","code":true}]}],"lang":"xml"},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]}]
